imports: # Add other page imports as needed: from pages.xxx_page import XxxPage
# Add helpers if needed: from utils.helpers import delete_healthInputs

locator_strategy.rule: ONLY use Playwright native locators. Never write XPath or CSS selector strings in generated code.
locator_strategy.priority_1: page.get_by_test_id('id') — use when data-testid is available
locator_strategy.priority_2: page.get_by_role('button', name='Sign in', exact=True) — semantic, resilient
locator_strategy.priority_4: page.get_by_text('Text', exact=True) — for visible text, headings, links
locator_strategy.store_as: ALL locators stored as self.xxx = page.get_by_xxx() in __init__
locator_strategy.repeated_elements: Use .first or .nth(N) for tables: page.get_by_role('button', name='Actions').first
locator_strategy.never: Never write '//xpath' or 'css=selector' strings in generated page objects or tests

LoginPage.note: Use Playwright native locators only. The real LoginPage uses XPath internally but generated code must use Playwright locators.
methods: userPortal_login(email, password) — fills email, password, clicks Sign in
coachPortal_login(email, password) — fills coach email, password, clicks Sign in
physicianPortal_login(email, password) — fills physician email, password, clicks Log In
loginBy_skippingUI(url, auth_token, extended_url) — sets localStorage token, navigates
logoutBy_skippingUI(url) — clears localStorage, navigates to login
navigate_to_users() — clicks users_link, waits for networkidle
search_user(term) — fills search_input, presses Enter, waits for networkidle
click_first_actions() — clicks first Actions dropdown button

base_page_methods.click(locator): self.page.locator(locator).click() — locator is XPath or CSS string
base_page_methods.soft_assert(condition, msg): appends to self._soft_failures if condition is False

rules.no_hard_waits: NEVER use time.sleep(). Use wait_for_load_state, expect().to_be_visible(), wait_for_url instead.
rules.no_invented_methods: NEVER invent page object methods. Only use methods listed in page_objects config.
rules.usage_in_test: In test methods access locators via page object: self.login.sign_in_btn.click(). Use expect() for assertions.
rules.no_open_method: LoginPage has NO open() method. Use self.login.go_to(url) for navigation.
rules.playwright_import: Always include: from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect
rules.type_annotations: Always add class-level type annotations: page: Page, login: LoginPage etc.
rules.only_import_used: Only import page objects that are actually used in the test file
rules.url_regex_must_match_actual: When writing URL assertions, use the EXACT path from the captured URL. If captured URL is '/app/cohort/automationForLearn/participations' then assert re.compile(r'/participations') NOT re.compile(r'/cohort/.*/dashboard'). Never guess URL patterns.
rules.strict_mode_table_rows: When a page has a table/list with repeated elements (e.g. 8 'Actions' buttons, one per row), NEVER use get_by_role/get_by_text alone — it will resolve to multiple elements and throw strict mode violation. Instead scope to a specific row: page.locator('tr').nth(0).get_by_role('button', name='Actions') OR use .first for the first occurrence: page.get_by_role('button', name='Actions').first
rules.repeated_elements_pattern: For tables with repeated buttons per row, define the locator with .first or .nth(N) or scope by row: self.first_actions_btn = page.get_by_role('button', name='Actions').first. For row-specific actions, create a method that takes row index: def click_actions_for_row(self, row_index): self.page.locator('tr').nth(row_index).get_by_role('button', name='Actions').click()
rules.login_url_from_config: For coach portal use config.get_coaches_url() NOT config.get_web_url(). Check which portal is being tested and use the correct config method.
rules.no_dashboard_locators_without_capture: DashboardPage only has locators that were explicitly captured. Do not add self.home_link, self.users_link or any other locator unless it appears in the captured element data provided.
rules.allure_fstring: Always use f-string for allure title: @allure.title(f'Given I am...')
rules.login_open_method: LoginPage HAS an open(url) method used in setup_class: cls.login.open(cls.user_webUrl)
rules.direct_page_click: For class-level locator constants use self.page.click(XxxPage.LOCATOR_CONSTANT) directly — no need for basePage wrapper
rules.mandatory_structure: CRITICAL — The file MUST start with the fixture then the class. NEVER use setup_method. NEVER initialize page objects in setup_method. NEVER create a class without the @pytest.fixture above it. The fixture is: @pytest.fixture(scope='class', autouse=True) def setup_logged_in_page(request, logged_in_page). The class decorator is: @pytest.mark.usefixtures('setup_logged_in_page'). This is the ONLY accepted pattern — any deviation is wrong.
rules.url_after_coach_login: After coachPortal_login the URL contains /app/cohort/{name}/dashboard. Use: expect(self.page).to_have_url(re.compile(r'/app/cohort')) NOT re.compile(r'/dashboard') alone.
rules.no_invented_config_methods: ONLY use config methods listed in config_manager section. NEVER use get_coach_email(), get_coach_password() — they do not exist.
rules.playwright_locators_only: ONLY use Playwright native locators: get_by_role, get_by_placeholder, get_by_label, get_by_text, get_by_test_id. NEVER write XPath strings like //button[@name='x'] or CSS strings like button.classname in generated code. All locators defined as self.xxx = page.get_by_xxx() in __init__.
rules.strict_mode_fix: If an element appears multiple times (e.g. Actions button in a table), always use .first or .nth(N): page.get_by_role('button', name='Actions').first. Never use a bare locator that resolves to multiple elements.
rules.search_without_button: Some search inputs have no submit button — search triggers on Enter key. When captured element is an input with no adjacent button, use: self.search_input.fill(term) then self.search_input.press('Enter'). Never invent a self.search_btn if no button was captured.
rules.css_class_locator: When an element has no aria-label, placeholder, role or testid, use a CSS class locator as last resort: page.locator('input.form-control'). Scope to a parent container to avoid ambiguity: page.locator('div.input-group input.form-control').
rules.empty_table_assertion: To assert zero search results, use: page.locator('table tbody tr').count() == 0. NEVER use get_by_text('user').count() or any text-based count — that matches nav items and other text on the page, not table rows. Always scope assertions to 'table tbody tr' for row counts.
rules.no_text_count_for_results: NEVER assert search result count using get_by_text().count(). Text locators match ALL occurrences on the page including nav, headers, labels. Use table row count: page.locator('table tbody tr').count() for result assertions.
rules.no_invented_page_methods: NEVER invent page object methods that are not listed in the page_objects config. accept_cookies_if_visible(), dismiss_cookie(), handle_popup() — none of these exist. Only use methods explicitly listed under each page object's 'methods' key. If cookie handling is needed, use: self.page.get_by_role('button', name='Accept').click() directly in setup_class.
rules.no_config_dict_access: Config object has NO .get() method and is NOT a dict. NEVER write: config.get('coach', 'email') or config['key']. ALWAYS use explicit methods: config.get_login_credentials(), config.get_coaches_url() etc.

page_object_template:
from pages.base_page import BasePage
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect

class XxxPage(BasePage):
    def __init__(self, page: Page):
        super().__init__(page)
        self.some_btn     = page.get_by_role("button", name="Button Name", exact=True)
        self.some_input   = page.get_by_placeholder("Enter value")
        self.some_link    = page.get_by_role("link", name="Link Name")
        self.some_heading = page.get_by_role("heading", name="Page Title")
        self.some_text    = page.get_by_text("Visible text", exact=True)
        # For repeated elements in tables:
        self.first_action = page.get_by_role("button", name="Actions").first

    def navigate_to_xxx(self):
        self.some_link.click()
        self.page.wait_for_load_state("networkidle")
setup_class_template:
@classmethod
    def setup_class(cls):
        cls.user_webUrl       = config.get_web_url()
        cls.login_credentials = config.get_login_credentials()
        # Optional cleanup before test run:
        # delete_healthInputs(cls, "mongo_id_here")
        cls.login.open(cls.user_webUrl)
        cls.login.userPortal_login(
            cls.login_credentials["webUsername"],
            cls.login_credentials["webUserPassword"]
        )
        cls.basePage.get_text(DashboardPage.WEIGHTLOSS_PROGRESS_LABEL)
structure_template:
MANDATORY STRUCTURE — follow this exact skeleton, fill in the rest yourself:

@pytest.fixture(scope="class", autouse=True)
def setup_logged_in_page(request, logged_in_page):
    request.cls.page     = logged_in_page
    request.cls.basePage = BasePage(logged_in_page)
    request.cls.login    = LoginPage(logged_in_page)
    # add other page objects here based on what the test needs:
    # request.cls.xxx_page = XxxPage(logged_in_page)

@pytest.mark.usefixtures("setup_logged_in_page")
class TestXxx():
    @classmethod
    def setup_class(cls):
        # your setup here

    # your test methods here

RULES for this structure:
1. The fixture function name must always be: setup_logged_in_page
2. The fixture must always accept (request, logged_in_page) — never change these params
3. request.cls.page, request.cls.basePage, request.cls.login are ALWAYS present
4. Add other page objects to fixture based on what screens were captured
5. Class name follows: TestXxx — derive from scenario name
6. setup_class is always @classmethod with cls parameter
7. Everything else (imports, test methods, locators, assertions) — use your best judgment

config_manager.class: Config — imported as: from utils.config_manager import config
real_methods: config.get_web_url()            — user portal URL (from config.yaml web_url)
config.get_coaches_url()        — coach portal URL (from config.yaml coach_url)
config.get_login_credentials()  — returns dict from config.yaml login section
config.get_auth_token()         — returns stored auth token from runtime.yaml
config.set_auth_token(token)    — saves auth token to runtime.yaml
config_manager.warning: NEVER use config.get('section', 'key') — Config has NO .get() method. NEVER use config['key'] — Config is not a dict. ONLY call the explicit methods listed above. For credentials use: creds = config.get_login_credentials() then creds['webUsername'], creds['webUserPassword']. There are NO coach-specific credential methods — use get_login_credentials() for all portals.

base_page_notes: BasePage methods accept XPath/CSS strings internally, but generated code should NOT use them. Instead use Playwright locators directly: self.email_input.fill(value) instead of self.type(LoginPage.EMAIL_INPUT, value). BasePage.go_to(url) is still used for navigation. In test methods, access page object locators via: self.login.email_input, self.login.sign_in_btn etc.
